evals.prompt: I just finished implementing the document upload feature with drag-and-drop in Upload.jsx. The backend endpoint is working but I haven't tested the frontend interaction at all. Can you set up Playwright and write e2e tests for the upload flow?
evals.expected_output: Playwright setup (config + install commands) and a spec file for the upload flow that reads Upload.jsx before writing selectors, tests file drop and the success/error feedback states.
assertions: Reads the Upload.jsx component source before writing selectors
Creates or references a playwright.config.ts file
Writes a spec file targeting the upload flow (drag-and-drop or file input)
Tests at least one happy path and one error case (e.g. unsupported file type)
Uses role/text/label selectors or reads component to get accurate class names — does NOT invent selectors like data-testid without verifying they exist

evals.prompt: I fixed a bug in the RagModal where the semantic score wasn't showing correctly. Existing e2e tests exist in front/e2e/. Run the rag-modal tests and tell me if we're good to commit.
evals.expected_output: Runs existing Playwright tests for the rag-modal, reports pass/fail results with specific error output if any, gives a clear commit/fix verdict.
assertions: Checks whether front/e2e/ and playwright.config.ts already exist before trying to set up
Runs the rag-modal tests (not all tests, just the relevant ones)
Gives a clear verdict: ready to commit or needs a fix first
Does NOT re-install Playwright if it's already configured

evals.prompt: Finished the TDD cycle for the chat sidebar. Red, green, refactor all done on the backend. Now I need to close out the frontend side — validate that creating and deleting chats works correctly in the browser.
evals.expected_output: Writes e2e tests for chat lifecycle (create + delete) by reading Sidebar.jsx first to get accurate selectors, runs them, reports result.
assertions: Reads Sidebar.jsx (or related component) before writing locators
Writes tests for both creating a chat and deleting a chat
Verifies that chat appears in list after creation and disappears after deletion
Runs the tests and reports the outcome
Handles the scenario where tests don't exist yet (writes them) rather than only trying to run them
